SEARCH
Package neqsim.standards.oilquality

Class Standard_ASTM_D611

java.lang.Object
neqsim.util.NamedBaseClass
neqsim.standards.Standard
neqsim.standards.oilquality.Standard_ASTM_D611
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable, StandardInterface, NamedInterface
public class Standard_ASTM_D611 extends Standard
Estimated Aniline Point - ASTM D611 (screening correlation).

The aniline point is the lowest temperature at which equal volumes of the oil and aniline are completely miscible. It is an inverse measure of the aromatic content of a distillate: paraffinic (high Watson K) fuels have high aniline points, aromatic stocks have low aniline points. It feeds the Diesel Index and is a component of jet-fuel and solvent quality control.

This class returns an estimate, not a laboratory measurement. The aniline point is physically governed by paraffinicity and boiling range, so it is estimated here from the Watson (UOP) characterization factor and the mean average boiling point (MeABP), both obtained internally from Standard_ASTM_D86. The default coefficients reproduce typical middle-distillate behaviour: 

 
 AP[C] = c0 + c1 * (Kw - 12.0) + c2 * (MeABP[C] - 190.0)

with defaults c0 = 60.0, c1 = 35.0, c2 = 0.083, anchored to: paraffinic kerosene (Kw ≈ 12.0, MeABP ≈ 190 °C → AP ≈ 60 °C), aromatic kerosene (Kw ≈ 11.0 → AP ≈ 25 °C) and paraffinic diesel (Kw ≈ 12.5, MeABP ≈ 280 °C → AP ≈ 85 °C). The coefficients are configurable so the correlation can be calibrated to measured assay data. For rigorous work, calibrate against ASTM D611 / API Procedure 2B8.1 (Walsh-Mortimer) data.

Usage example: 

 
 Standard_ASTM_D611 aniline = new Standard_ASTM_D611(dieselFluid);
 aniline.calculate();
 double anilinePointC = aniline.getValue("anilinePoint", "C");
Version:
1.0
Author:
ESOL

    • setCorrelationCoefficients

      public void setCorrelationCoefficients(double newC0, double newC1, double newC2)
      Sets the correlation coefficients used to estimate the aniline point.
      Parameters:
      newC0 - intercept coefficient (default 60.0)
      newC1 - Watson-K coefficient (default 35.0)
      newC2 - boiling-point coefficient (default 0.083)

    • setMinAnilineSpec

      public void setMinAnilineSpec(double minAniline, String minAnilineUnit)
      Sets an optional minimum aniline-point specification limit used by isOnSpec().
      Parameters:
      minAniline - minimum allowed aniline point
      minAnilineUnit - temperature unit, one of "C", "K", "F", "R"

    • clearMinAnilineSpec

      public void clearMinAnilineSpec()
      Clears any previously configured minimum aniline-point specification limit.

    • convertTempFromC

      private double convertTempFromC(double valueC, String unit)
      Converts a temperature from Celsius to the requested unit.
      Parameters:
      valueC - temperature value in Celsius (may be NaN)
      unit - target unit, one of "C", "K", "F", "R"
      Returns:
      the converted temperature, or the Celsius value if the unit is unrecognised

    • convertTempToC

      private double convertTempToC(double value, String unit)
      Converts a temperature in the supplied unit to Celsius.
      Parameters:
      value - temperature value
      unit - source unit, one of "C", "K", "F", "R"
      Returns:
      the temperature in Celsius
